HolidayPackage.title: HolidayPackage
HolidayPackage.description: Represents a single holiday, cruise, or deal package offered by Elite Escapes, including all details necessary for display and search.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HolidayPackage entity.

title.description: A compelling, conversion-optimized headline for the holiday package, potentially AI-generated (e.g., 'Your Dream Bali Escape').

description.description: A detailed description of the holiday package, highlighting key features, inclusions, and experiences.

type.description: The type of travel package, categorizing it as a holiday, cruise, or special deal for filtering and display.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image representing the holiday package for display in listings and detail pages.

rating.description: Average customer rating for the holiday package (e.g., 4.8 out of 5), derived from an external system or static.

destinationId.description: Reference to the Destination entity associated with this package. (Relationship: Destination 1:N HolidayPackage)

hotelName.description: The name of the primary hotel or accommodation included in the package.

durationNights.description: The total number of nights included in the holiday package.

boardType.description: The type of board basis for the accommodation (e.g., 'All Inclusive', 'Half Board', 'Bed & Breakfast').

priceOriginal.description: The original price of the package before any discounts, used for strikethrough display in 'Last Minute Offers'.

priceCurrent.description: The current selling price of the holiday package after any applicable discounts.

discountAmountText.description: Text describing the discount amount (e.g., 'Save £200'), for display on special offers.

isSpecialOffer.description: Indicates if the package is a special offer, for displaying a 'Special Offer' badge.

departureFrom.description: The primary departure airport or location for the package (e.g., 'London Gatwick').

availableStartDate.description: The earliest date from which this package is available for booking or travel.

availableEndDate.description: The latest date until which this package is available for booking or travel.

minGuests.description: The minimum number of guests allowed for this package booking.

maxGuests.description: The maximum number of guests allowed for this package booking.

isFeatured.description: Flag to indicate if this package should appear in the 'Featured Holidays' section on the homepage.

isLastMinute.description: Flag to indicate if this package should appear in the 'Last Minute Offers' section on the homepage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the holiday package record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the holiday package record was last updated.

Destination.title: Destination
Destination.description: Represents a geographical location or destination for travel, often featured for popular holidays.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Destination entity.

name.description: The common name of the destination (e.g., 'Maldives', 'Paris').

description.description: A brief description or captivating text for the destination, often used as an overlay on images.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the destination, used in destination cards.

country.description: The country where the destination is located.

continent.description: The continent where the destination is located.

isPopular.description: Flag to indicate if this destination should be featured in the 'Popular Destinations' section on the homepage.

BranchLocation.title: BranchLocation
BranchLocation.description: Represents a physical branch office of Elite Escapes, allowing users to find local support and services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BranchLocation entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the branch location (e.g., 'London Oxford Street Branch').

addressLine1.description: The first line of the branch's street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the branch's street address, if applicable.

city.description: The city where the branch is located.

postcode.description: The postcode or zip code of the branch location.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the branch.

email.description: The email address for inquiries specific to this branch, if available.

openingHoursDescription.description: A human-readable description of the branch's opening hours (e.g., 'Mon-Fri: 9am-5pm, Sat: 10am-4pm').

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the branch, useful for map integration.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the branch, useful for map integration.

mapLink.description: A URL to a map service showing the branch location.

NewsletterSubscriber.title: NewsletterSubscriber
NewsletterSubscriber.description: Represents a user who has subscribed to Elite Escapes' newsletter for updates and promotional offers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsletterSubscriber entity.

email.description: The email address of the newsletter subscriber.

subscriptionDate.description: Timestamp when the user subscribed to the newsletter.

optInMarketing.description: Indicates if the subscriber has explicitly opted in for additional marketing communications beyond the newsletter.

definition.description: Stores all holiday, cruise, and deal packages offered by Elite Escapes. Designed for public read access and backend/admin write access. Includes denormalized 'isFeatured', 'isLastMinute', 'destinationId', and 'type' fields to support Query as Permission (QAP) for efficient filtering and display.

params.name: holidayPackageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific holiday package.

definition.description: Stores geographical locations or destinations for travel. Designed for public read access and backend/admin write access. Includes the 'isPopular' field for QAP, enabling efficient listing of popular destinations.

params.name: destinationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific destination.

definition.description: Stores details of Elite Escapes' physical branch offices. Designed for public read access and backend/admin write access to allow users to find local support.

params.name: branchLocationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific branch location.

definition.description: Stores email addresses of users who subscribe to the newsletter. Designed to allow anonymous 'create' operations. Read, update, and delete access are restricted to an administrative backend to ensure privacy and data integrity.

params.name: subscriberId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific newsletter subscriber, typically a client-generated ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore data structure for Elite Escapes prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization rules by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The design primarily leverages structural segregation and a clear access model to achieve Authorization Independence and robust Query as Permission (QAP) capabilities.

**Authorization Independence:** For the majority of content entities (`HolidayPackage`, `Destination`, `BranchLocation`), authorization independence is inherently achieved by designing these collections for **public read access**. This eliminates the need for complex `get()` operations in security rules, as any user (authenticated or unauthenticated) can read the data without dependencies on other documents or user-specific attributes for authorization. Write operations for these public collections are assumed to be handled by an administrative backend system with elevated privileges (e.g., using a service account) or by authenticated users with specific roles defined in a separate database collection, thereby maintaining authorization independence. For the `NewsletterSubscriber` collection, authorization independence is maintained by allowing **anonymous write access** for `create` operations. Each subscription is an independent document. Read, update, and delete operations for newsletter subscribers are restricted to an administrative system, ensuring user privacy and preventing any complex inter-document dependencies for user-level authorization.

**QAPs (Queries as Permissions):**
*   **`/holiday_packages/{holidayPackageId}`:** This collection is optimized for efficient `list` operations. Fields such as `isFeatured`, `isLastMinute`, `destinationId`, and `type` are directly queryable, enabling the front-end to display sections like "Featured Holidays" and "Last Minute Offers" without requiring security rules to filter data. All documents in this collection share the same public read security posture, making `allow read: true;` rules simple and robust.
*   **`/destinations/{destinationId}`:** Similar to holiday packages, this collection supports QAPs for sections like "Popular Destinations" by making the `isPopular` field directly queryable. All destination data is publicly readable.
*   **`/branch_locations/{branchLocationId}`:** This collection supports simple listing queries for all branch locations, as all documents are publicly readable.
*   **`/newsletter_subscribers/{subscriberId}`:** This collection's primary QAP is for client-side `create` operations by any user. No public `list` or `read` access is granted, ensuring privacy and preventing unauthorized data exposure through security rules.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   All public-facing content (`holiday_packages`, `destinations`, `branch_locations`) is strictly segregated into its own top-level collections. This ensures a homogeneous security posture (public read) across all documents within each of these collections, simplifying rule writing.
*   User-submitted data (`newsletter_subscribers`) is placed in a separate collection, allowing for precise and distinct security rules (anonymous create, admin-only read/update/delete) without impacting the access model of the public content.

**Access Modeling & Predictability:**
*   Collection paths and wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `/holiday_packages/{holidayPackageId}`), enhancing clarity and debuggability.
*   The design avoids deeply nested user-owned data structures, simplifying path-based authorization. For `NewsletterSubscriber`, the `subscriberId` (document ID) is a generated identifier, and the `email` field is stored within the document, ensuring that uniqueness of the email is handled by a robust backend or through specific, non-dependent rules.

This architecture results in concise, clear, and easily auditable security rules, minimizing the risk of security vulnerabilities and greatly simplifying debugging and future maintenance.
